summary: Removed the repository-wide catch-all headers `include/functions.h`, `include/variables.h`, and `include/global.h`, replacing their implicit declarations/includes with more localized module headers and explicit system/library declarations across a large set of files. The PR mainly improved header hygiene and dependency locality, while also tightening some item/fighter-related function signatures and adding build-script conveniences.
what_changed: Deleted `include/functions.h`, a 903-line monolithic prototype bucket grouped by subsystems such as lbCollision, cmCamera, ftFighter, ftCommon, items, Dolphin/MSL, and sysdolphin.
Deleted `include/variables.h` and `include/global.h`; `global.h` had only pulled in Dolphin types, GX/MTX types, `functions.h`, and `variables.h`.
Removed many `#include <global.h>`, `#include <functions.h>`, and `#include <variables.h>` dependencies and replaced them with narrower includes or declarations in subsystem headers.

Added or expanded many local headers to hold declarations closer to their owning modules, including fighter, item, camera, effects, ground, player, library, MSL, Dolphin MTX/VI, and sysdolphin baselib headers.
Added `src/melee/it/item2.h` with declarations for `func_8026B9A8`, `func_8026B2B4`, `itGetKind`, `func_8026B30C`, `func_8026B320`, and nearby item helpers.

Adjusted item signatures away from broad integer placeholders where evidence indicated object pointers or byte-sized parameters, for example `func_8026B9A8(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, u8)`, `func_8026AB54(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, u8)`, `func_8026B6A8(Vec3*, HSD_GObj*)`, and `func_802742F4(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, u8)`.

Added standard/library prototypes to their more appropriate headers, such as runtime conversion helpers in `include/Runtime/runtime.h`, string functions in `include/string.h`, PSMTX functions in `include/dolphin/mtx.h`, and `VIWaitForRetrace` in `include/dolphin/vi/vi.h`.

In `src/melee/it/item2.c`, included the new `melee/it/item2.h` and `melee/ft/ftlib.h`, updated extern signatures, converted `func_8026B684` to pass `NULL` to a pointer-typed helper, and documented item-camera helper uncertainty with a `@todo`.
Kept an inline assembly fallback for `func_8026B634` behind `#if FALSE` / `#else`, with a comment noting a missing third argument passed to `func_80086368`.
Updated `build.sh` to add a bash shebang, `-j` job count support, `-p` formatting support via `make format`, and map parsing when `GALE01.map` exists rather than only during dump mode.
smart_moves: Removed the catch-all declaration headers instead of continuing to let files compile through implicit global transitive dependencies.
Moved declarations into subsystem-owned headers, making future decomp work less likely to rely on stale or misleading prototypes from a central dumping ground.

Used the header cleanup as an opportunity to refine suspicious signatures from `s32` placeholders to more meaningful types such as `HSD_GObj*` and `u8` where the diff showed callers/signatures demanded it.
Added a new `item2.h` rather than keeping `item2.c` declarations in the large `item.h` or a global header.

Preserved matching progress pragmatically: when the C version of `func_8026B634` had an unresolved signature issue, the PR left an inline assembly fallback and documented the missing argument instead of silently relying on a fake extern.
Added build ergonomics (`-j`, optional formatting, automatic map parsing when present) that help large header churn be iterated and verified faster.
decomp_lessons: Catch-all headers are dangerous in decomp projects because they hide real dependencies and allow incorrect prototypes to keep compiling.
When deleting a global header, move declarations to the smallest plausible owner header and add explicit type includes instead of recreating the same transitive dependency under another name.
Prototype corrections can affect matching: a signature change exposed that an item function had been relying on an `extern` to fakematch.
After a signature/include change appears to break a match, re-test before permanently marking code nonmatching; the reviewer found that removing the proposed nonmatching guard still matched.
Prefer pointer-typed declarations and explicit `NULL` for object arguments over `s32`/`0` placeholders once evidence supports the real type.
If a helper call has an unresolved hidden/missing argument, document it with `@todo` and isolate any asm fallback clearly rather than obscuring the issue in a fake C signature.
Large header cleanups should include related platform/library prototypes in their canonical headers, such as MSL string/trig functions, Dolphin MTX/VI routines, and runtime helpers.
naming_conventions: New module header include guard style observed: `MELEE_IT_ITEM2_H` for `src/melee/it/item2.h`.
Subsystem headers use include paths such as `<melee/it/item2.h>`, `<melee/ft/ftlib.h>`, and `<sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h>` rather than global project headers.
Doxygen-style comments were used for migrated declarations and uncertain functions, including `@brief` and `@todo`.
The code continued to use address-derived placeholder names such as `func_8026B9A8`, but improved parameter names/types where possible, e.g. `item_gobj`, `HSD_GObj*`, and `u8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Avoid `extern` fakematches: the review discussion explicitly says `func_8026B9A8` stopped matching after a signature changed because it had been using an extern to fakematch.
Do not add `NON_MATCHING` solely because a signature churn appears to break a function; reviewer `r-burns` asked to retry, and the author confirmed removing the nonmatching guard still matched.

For `func_8026B634`, the diff changed `func_80086368` from a two-argument extern to `func_80086368(Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32)` and kept an inline asm implementation because the C version was missing the third argument.
Use `#pragma push` / `#pragma pop` and `// clang-format off` around inline asm fallbacks to keep assembly isolated and formatting-stable.
Changing `func_800864A8` to take `HSD_GObj*` let callers use `NULL` and object pointers instead of integer placeholders.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned why a `NON_MATCHING` guard was needed around `func_8026B9A8`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Explained that the function appeared to stop matching after a signature changed because it had been using an `extern` to fakematch.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Asked the author to try again because removing the nonmatching guard still matched locally.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue migrating declarations out of broad headers into subsystem headers.
Check whether `func_8026B634` was later rematched in C after resolving the missing third argument to `func_80086368`.
Search for remaining `extern` fakematches that rely on deliberately wrong signatures.
Audit remaining declarations that still use `s32` where call sites suggest `HSD_GObj*` or `u8`.
Look for later cleanup of duplicated item declarations between `item.h` and `item2.h`.
